title: China-to-Kenya Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T and NB/T Baseline vs Kenya Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Kenya acceptance is controlled by KEBS import conformity and by EPRA or Kenya Power project approval where the inverter is grid-connected. A China GB/T or NB/T report can support technical due diligence, but the target-market decision depends on Kenya standards, approved specifications, PVoC contractor review, and project-specific grid requirements.
target_standards: KEBS PVoC and applicable Kenya Standards or approved specifications
EPRA electricity and renewable-energy regulatory instruments
Kenya Power site-specific connection requirements
rows.china_equivalent: The China baseline commonly includes GB/T 37408 for PV inverter technical requirements, GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station grid connection, NB/T 32004 for PV grid-connected inverter requirements, and voluntary CQC or CGC certification. These documents are not a Kenya market-access approval unless accepted through the Kenya route.
china_standards: CQC / CGC voluntary certification rules where applicable
rows.gap: The equivalence gap is legal recognition. Build a crosswalk from each Chinese test clause to IEC 62109, Kenya standards or approved specifications, and Kenya Power settings; flag any missing anti-islanding, lab accreditation, language, label, or firmware evidence before PVoC filing.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only through Kenya import or grid-approval routes; Kenya acceptance must be confirmed by KEBS, the PVoC contractor, EPRA, Kenya Power, or the project owner as applicable
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T and NB/T evidence is China-market or voluntary/project evidence and is not a Kenya approval by itself

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB/T and NB/T files are useful source evidence, but the Kenya deliverable should be a standards crosswalk plus PVoC and grid-connection evidence accepted by the relevant Kenyan authority or project counterparty.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform (China)

rows.requirement: Kenya 50 Hz Grid Settings and Protection Windows
rows.target_requirement: Kenya's public supply is operated as an AC utility system; Kenya Power is the distribution utility for customer supply and connection. For PV inverter projects, the export model should be configured and documented for the Kenya site's nominal 50 Hz grid and the frequency, voltage, trip, reconnect, and ride-through windows required by the connection authority or project specification.
target_standards: Kenya Power connection and supply requirements
Site-specific grid-code or distribution-code settings issued by the connection authority
Inverter protection and anti-islanding settings for nominal 50 Hz operation
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses nominal 50 Hz AC grids, so the headline frequency is not the main difference. The gap is the allowed protection windows, reconnect timing, voltage ride-through, and anti-islanding parameters accepted by Kenya Power or the Kenya project, which may differ from Chinese defaults.
rows.gap: The settings gap is small on nominal frequency but material on acceptance values. Lock the Kenya firmware profile, document all protection thresholds, and make installer access controls clear so China-market defaults are not accidentally commissioned in Kenya.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a connection and commissioning requirement where Kenya Power, EPRA, project owner, or installer acceptance specifies frequency and protection settings
mandatory_status.cn: Nominal 50 Hz is common, but Chinese protection profiles are not automatically valid for Kenya

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Nominal 50 Hz compatibility is necessary but not enough. Kenya-facing inverter files should show the exact grid profile and protection settings accepted for the connection point.

source.publisher: Kenya Power and Lighting Company PLC

rows.requirement: EPRA Licensing and Regulatory Route for Grid-Connected Solar PV
rows.target_requirement: EPRA is Kenya's electricity and renewable-energy regulator and publishes electricity and renewable-energy regulatory instruments and application guidelines. A solar PV inverter is not cleared for a grid-connected project merely by import PVoC; the project developer or customer must still follow the applicable EPRA licence, exemption, registration, net-metering, tariff, and technical-regulatory route for the generating facility and use case. User-supplied flags for the Energy (Net-Metering) Regulations, 2024 (Legal Notice No. 104) should be verified against EPRA or Kenya Power before relying on stated capacity limits or credit values.
target_standards: Energy Act, 2019

Energy (Net-Metering) Regulations, 2024 (Legal Notice No. 104) - verify current text before relying on user-supplied 4 kW single-phase, 10 kW three-phase, 1 MW commercial/industrial, or approximate 50% retail-tariff credit figures
EPRA electricity regulatory instruments and application guidelines
EPRA renewable-energy regulatory instruments and application guidelines
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic grid acceptance is normally handled through Chinese grid-company and project rules, with documents such as GB/T 19964-2024, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, and utility-specific settings. Those records do not establish EPRA licensing status or Kenya project eligibility.
rows.gap: The project gap is regulatory ownership. Confirm whether the Kenya installation is captive, embedded, distributed, commercial, utility-scale, or exempt; then align inverter anti-islanding, protection, metering, and commissioning evidence to the EPRA route and connection authority.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the Energy Act, EPRA licensing rules, tariff rules, or project approval route applies; may be exempt or registration-based for smaller systems depending on current EPRA rules
mandatory_status.cn: Not applicable for Kenya projects; Chinese domestic grid approvals do not replace EPRA review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Separate the product import file from the Kenya project approval file. EPRA and the connection authority decide the grid-connected use case; a PVoC CoC alone does not approve generation or export to the grid.

source.publisher: Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA), Kenya

rows.requirement: Kenya Power Connection and Net-Metering Readiness
rows.target_requirement: Kenya Power is the national distribution utility and the practical connection counterparty for many customer-side PV systems. Net-metering or export arrangements require the applicable EPRA framework, Kenya Power connection review, metering arrangement, protection settings, and commissioning acceptance. The Energy (Net-Metering) Regulations, 2024 (Legal Notice No. 104) and user-supplied figures such as 4 kW single-phase, 10 kW three-phase, 1 MW commercial/industrial, and an approximate 50% retail-tariff credit should be treated as verification points unless the current EPRA/Kenya Power text is in the project file. An inverter should therefore have anti-islanding, export-control, protection, and meter-interface documentation ready for the site-specific application.
target_standards: Kenya Power customer connection and supply procedures
Energy (Net-Metering) Regulations, 2024 (Legal Notice No. 104) - current eligibility, capacity limits, and credit formula should be verified with EPRA or Kenya Power
EPRA net-metering, tariff, and renewable-energy framework where applicable
Site-specific interconnection agreement, metering arrangement, and commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection reports may demonstrate anti-islanding and protection functions, but their default voltage, frequency, reconnect, ramp-rate, and export-control settings may not match the Kenya Power site. Kenya-specific parameter sheets and commissioning records should be prepared separately.
rows.gap: The connection gap is site-specific settings. Prepare Kenya Power-facing single-line diagrams, inverter protection settings, anti-islanding test evidence, export limitation logic where required, meter wiring diagrams, and installer commissioning forms.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection, export, or net-metering only where the site seeks those arrangements; off-grid or non-export systems may follow different electrical and customer requirements
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid approval is not accepted as Kenya Power connection approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not ship with only a generic grid certificate if the Kenya project will export power. Build a Kenya Power and EPRA connection pack with model-specific protection, anti-islanding, metering, and commissioning evidence.

source.publisher: Kenya Power and Lighting Company PLC

rows.requirement: KEBS PVoC Import Conformity for Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: KEBS operates the Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) programme for goods destined to Kenya. The official KEBS page states that PVoC assesses products in the exporting country against applicable Kenyan technical regulations, mandatory standards, or approved specifications; China is listed in the PVoC contractor zone. For a solar PV inverter shipment, the importer and exporter should plan for a Certificate of Conformity or applicable exemption before Kenyan port clearance.
target_standards: KEBS Pre-Export Verification of Conformity to Standards (PVoC)
The Verification of Conformity to Kenya Standards of Imports (Amendment) Order, 2020 (Legal Notice No. 78 of 2020)
Applicable Kenya Standards or approved specifications for the declared inverter model
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin factory reports, CQC or CGC certificates, CE files, and IEC/CB reports may support the PVoC review, but they are not themselves the Kenya PVoC Certificate of Conformity. The PVoC agent still reviews the shipment, model documents, standards mapping, and inspection route for Kenya.
china_standards: Voluntary CQC or CGC PV inverter certification evidence where available
rows.gap: The export-file gap is route control: Kenya requires the PVoC import conformity route unless a valid exemption applies. Prepare the IDF, invoice, packing list, HS code, model list, nameplate ratings, test reports, user manual, and Kenyan standard or approved-specification mapping before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for imported goods subject to Kenya PVoC unless a KEBS-recognised exemption or waiver applies
mandatory_status.cn: Not an outbound China certification requirement; China documents may support Kenya PVoC but do not replace it

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Kenya PVoC as the import gate for China-origin solar PV inverters unless KEBS confirms an exemption. A China certificate package should be prepared as supporting evidence, not as a substitute for the Kenyan CoC route.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence for PV Inverter PVoC Files
rows.target_requirement: KEBS says PVoC certification is based on Kenya standards or approved specifications. For PV inverters, import conformity files commonly need model-specific safety evidence aligned to IEC 62109-1 for general converter safety and IEC 62109-2 for inverter-specific requirements, or to any Kenya adoption or approved specification required by the PVoC contractor. The mandatory act is the Kenya import conformity decision; IEC 62109 evidence is the technical basis when requested or mapped by the applicable standard route.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 - Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems - General requirements
IEC 62109-2 - Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems - Particular requirements for inverters
Applicable Kenya Standard adoption or approved specification used by the PVoC contractor
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter files often include GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, GB/T 19964-2024, and voluntary certification reports. These can be cross-referenced to IEC safety and grid topics, but the PVoC agent may require IEC/CB reports, Kenya-adopted standards, or retesting if the Chinese report does not match the declared Kenya route.
rows.gap: The standards gap is traceability. Match the exact inverter model, firmware, ratings, protective functions, labels, and manuals to the IEC 62109 or Kenya-approved specification used in the PVoC file; do not rely on a generic China grid report alone.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only through the applicable Kenya PVoC, Kenya Standard, approved-specification, customer, or project route; IEC 62109 itself should be confirmed for the declared model and shipment
mandatory_status.cn: Usually voluntary or project/customer evidence for export unless tied to a Chinese domestic certification or procurement requirement

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 evidence as a likely safety backbone for the PVoC file, but confirm the exact Kenya standard or approved specification with the appointed PVoC contractor before shipment.
